topic: A student discovers their teacher follows them on social media
raw_output:
[story 1]

The late afternoon light slanted across Liam’s desk, illuminating dust motes dancing in the air. He was scrolling through his feed, mindlessly consuming updates, when a notification popped up. It was from Professor Albright. A picture. It was a candid shot, taken seemingly in a busy coffee shop, smiling warmly. Liam froze, his thumb hovering over the screen. He hadn't seen Professor Albright outside of the classroom in months, and this photo felt… intimate. A cold prickle traced its way down his spine. He quickly minimized the app, his heart thumping a sudden, irregular rhythm against his ribs. He tried to focus on his calculus homework, but the lines swam before his eyes. Every time he glanced toward the door, he half-expected to see a familiar, knowing gaze watching him from the hallway.

[story 2]

Maya leaned back in her chair, trying to appear absorbed in her textbook, but her attention was fractured. Her phone buzzed with an Instagram notification. It was a story from Mr. Harrison—her notoriously stern history teacher. The post showed a snippet of him laughing, a moment of unguarded joy that felt entirely out of character. Maya’s breath hitched. She felt a strange tightness in her chest, like someone had just subtly tightened a vice around her lungs. She deliberately turned her body away from the screen, focusing instead on the dense text about the Industrial Revolution. When she dared to look up again, she saw a familiar profile picture overlaid on the corner of the screen—her own profile picture, subtly cropped, positioned near his. A shiver ran down her arms, making her grip the pen so tightly her knuckles turned white.

[story 3]

“Did you see the announcement?” Sarah asked, her voice sounding strangely high-pitched. She was staring at her laptop screen, which displayed a recent post from Dr. Evans, her literature professor. It was a picture of a campus event, but in the background, slightly blurred, was a familiar corner of Sarah’s dorm room. The realization hit her like a physical blow. She blinked rapidly, trying to rationalize the coincidence, but the image persisted—Dr. Evans, seemingly present at a gathering she hadn't even known about, and somehow, connected to her personal space. Sarah stood up abruptly, knocking her chair slightly off balance. The scrape of the wood against the floor sounded deafening in the quiet library, and she couldn't stop the frantic, shallow breaths she was taking.
